articles.title: MotoGP: Di Giannantonio wins dramatic Catalan GP after major crashes
articles.description: Fabio Di Giannantonio won a chaotic, red-flagged Catalan GP, his first victory since 2023. The race was marred by high-profile crashes from Jorge Martin, Pedro Acosta, Alex Marquez, and Johann Zarco, leading to multiple restarts.

articles.description: Legendary Nepali climber Kami Rita Sherpa has set a new world record, scaling Mount Everest for the 32nd time on May 17. On the same day, Lhakpa Sherpa, the "Mountain Queen," also broke her own record for the most ascents by a female, reaching the summit for the 11th time.

articles.title: Microsoft is retiring Teams’ Together Mode in favour of ...
articles.description: Microsoft is retiring its Together Mode feature in Teams on June 30, opting for a streamlined meeting experience centered on the Gallery view. This consolidation aims to simplify interfaces, reduce complexity, and enhance video quality and performance for all users. The move promises fewer clicks and smoother video, especially on less powerful devices, allowing for faster innovation in core features.

articles.title: How Sophie Cunningham became the WNBA’s newest fashion favorite while dominating on court
articles.description: Sophie Cunningham is becoming one of the WNBA’s fastest-rising personalities thanks to her growing influence both on and off the court. The Indiana Fever guard recently impressed in a win over the Seattle Storm while continuing to gain attention through fashion appearances, social media and media projects. Her Sports Illustrated Swimsuit debut strengthened her public image, adding to a career that already includes strong performances, brand partnerships and leadership within Indiana’s roster after recovering from injury.

articles.title: Patrick Mahomes' latest injury update leaves Chiefs facing serious Week 1 uncertainty
articles.description: Patrick Mahomes’ recovery from a torn ACL and LCL has become a major concern ahead of the Kansas City Chiefs’ Week 1 game against the Denver Broncos. Former NFL physician David J. Chao warned that Mahomes will not be fully healthy by the opener and may struggle with mobility. While coach Andy Reid praised Mahomes’ rehab progress, the Chiefs remain uncertain about his availability. The situation has sparked growing anxiety around Kansas City’s 2026 season hopes.
